params._opcmAddress: Address of the OPContractsManager contract to verify.

params._addr: Address of the contract to verify.
params._name: Name of the contract to verify.

run().notice: Entry point for the script when run via `forge script`, reads the OPCM address from         the environment variable OPCM_ADDRESS. Use run(address) if you want to specify the         address as an argument instead. Running in this mode will not allow you to skip         constructor verification.

runSingle(string,address,bool).notice: Entry point for the script when trying to verify a single contract by name.

run().notice: Entry point for the script when run via `forge script`, reads the OPCM address from         the environment variable OPCM_ADDRESS. Use run(address) if you want to specify the         address as an argument instead. Running in this mode will not allow you to skip         constructor verification.

runSingle(string,address,bool).notice: Entry point for the script when trying to verify a single contract by name.

FfiFailed(string).notice: Error for when an ffi command fails.

VerifyOPCM_ContractsContainerMismatch().notice: Thrown when contractsContainer addresses are not the same across all OPCM components.

VerifyOPCM_CreationBytecodeNotFound(stri.notice: Thrown when the creation bytecode is not found in an artifact file.

VerifyOPCM_EmptyArtifactFile(string).notice: Thrown when an artifact file is empty.

VerifyOPCM_Failed().notice: Thrown when the top-level verification fails.

VerifyOPCM_NoBlueprints().notice: Thrown when no blueprints are found in the OPCM.

VerifyOPCM_NoImplementations().notice: Thrown when no implementations are found in the OPCM.

VerifyOPCM_NoProperties().notice: Thrown when no properties are found in the OPCM.

VerifyOPCM_RuntimeBytecodeNotFound(strin.notice: Thrown when the runtime bytecode is not found in an artifact file.

VerifyOPCM_UnaccountedGetters(string[]).notice: Thrown when there are getter functions in the ABI that are not being checked.

VerifyOPCM_UnexpectedPart().notice: Thrown when an unexpected part number is found in the blueprint.

userdoc.notice: Verifies the bytecode of an OPContractsManager instance and all associated blueprints         and implementations against locally built artifacts.

params._opcmAddress: Address of the OPContractsManager contract to verify.

params._addr: Address of the contract to verify.
params._name: Name of the contract to verify.

@notice Maps expected getter function names to their verification method.
 Value can be either:
 - An environment variable name (e.g., "EXPECTED_SUPERCHAIN_CONFIG") for getters verified via env vars
 - "SKIP" for getters verified elsewhere in the verification process
 WARNING: Do NOT add new getters without understanding their verification method!
@notice Setup flag.
